title: Lynn teacher was drugged and assaulted by coworker, lawsuit claims
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A Lynn teacher alleges she was drugged and sexually assaulted by a male coworker who had been acquitted of prior charges.
The teacher's lawsuit claims Lynn Public Schools failed to act quickly and kept the alleged assailant in the same building.
The lawsuit, seeking over $500,000, states the drink contained Rohypnol, GHB, and ketamine, though official confirmation was delayed.

title: Exonerated Chelsea man is suing for $1 million after 34 years in prison
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Thomas Rosa Jr., exonerated after 34 years in prison, sues Massachusetts for $1 million maximum compensation.
New DNA evidence led to vacating previous convictions due to unreliable eyewitness testimony, preventing a retrial.
Rosa's lawsuit alleges police misconduct, fabricated evidence, and destruction of exonerating evidence.

title: Ranking the Patriots’ 2026 schedule from least to most exciting games
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots' 2026 schedule includes games against eight 2025 playoff teams, including the Seahawks, Chargers, and Chiefs.
New England will play international and West Coast games, including a match in Germany against the Detroit Lions.
The Patriots have the sixth-hardest 2026 NFL schedule, with a .531 opponents' winning percentage from 2025.

title: Lakeville police investigating pipe bomb found near town pond
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A resident discovered a PVC pipe bomb near Egger Bog Pond off Highland Road in Lakeville.
The Massachusetts State Police Bomb Squad confirmed and disposed of the pipe bomb safely.
Lakeville police are investigating and urge anyone with information to contact Detective Robert Schiffer.

title: Mass. McDonald’s employee to face criminal charges after viral video shows alleged food contamination
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A Southbridge McDonald's employee is facing criminal charges after a video showed alleged food tampering with french fries.
The viral video appeared to show the employee eating fries before returning them to a container, leading to her dismissal.
The Southbridge franchise owners stated no public health concerns were found and emphasized their commitment to safety standards.

title: Boston man awaiting trial for allegedly dumping woman’s body found guilty of rape, strangulation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rinnyers Pena, 47, was found guilty on six counts, including rape, strangulation, and drugging for sexual intercourse.
Pena was acquitted of kidnapping and two counts of assault and battery, related to separate indictments from 2021 and 2025.
Pena faces a separate charge for disinterring a body, connected to Alenny Matos's 2020 death, with a plea deal uncertain.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 2-1 win over Minnesota United
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution defeated Minnesota United 2-1 at Gillette Stadium with goals from Carles Gil and Luca Langoni.
Matt Turner made crucial saves for the Revolution, particularly in the closing stages of the match against Minnesota.
Luca Langoni's decisive goal came from a counterattack, illustrating New England's aggressive play under coach Marko Mitrović.

title: SNL recap: Best moments from Will Ferrell hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’ season finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Will Ferrell hosted the 'Saturday Night Live' season 51 finale on May 16, with Paul McCartney as the musical guest.
The cold open featured Ferrell as Jeffrey Epstein's ghost, showing Donald Trump a future vision of his cabinet.
Paul McCartney performed three songs, including 'Days We Left Behind' and 'Band on the Run', and appeared in sketches.

title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 will host the Sail250 fleet featuring ships from over 25 countries from July 11 to 16.
Previous Sail Boston events occurred in 1976, 1980, 1992, 2000, 2009, 2012, and 2017 with millions of spectators.
Sail Boston 2017 featured the Eagle, a U.S. training ship originally seized from Nazi Germany after World War II.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots' morale improving as Mike Vrabel returns to form during rookie minicamp, despite off-field controversy.
Mike Vrabel's energy likened to last season's as Patriots aim for a successful 2026, preparing for tough opponents.
Kevin Byard reassures Vrabel's consistency and influence; team focuses on enhancing talent, potentially acquiring A.J. Brown.
